title: cyanus pinnatifidus subsp. sooanus
intro: Cyanus pinnatifidus subsp. sooanus, also known as Soo's field scabious, is a rare and subspecies of Cyanus pinnatifidus, a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Soo's field scabious include its ability to soothe and heal skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for skin health. Herbal preparations of Soo's field scabious often involve infusing the dried flowers in hot water to create a tea, or using the plant's extract in creams and ointments to apply topically.
